metadata.description: Standards bypass configuration for this branch

bypass.reason: handle_command() depth 4 — standard command dispatcher pattern, depth is horizontal if/elif not vertical nesting
track_usage() depth 4 — clean linear logic, depth from defensive try-except
api_call_with_retry() depth 5 — standard retry-with-backoff pattern, nesting is inherent to retry loops

3 functions: get_caller_info() depth 5 (stack inspection), get_json_folder_path() depth 5 (caller pattern matching), detect_caller_category() depth 4 (path-to-category mapping) — all inherent complexity
make_api_request() depth 4 — standard retry pattern with error handling
2 functions: _read_key_from_secrets() depth 5 (file read with validation guards), get_key_from_config() depth 4 (config navigation with nested dict structure)
Imports get_api_key from auth/keys — same-branch handler cross-package import needed for API key retrieval before making requests
Imports get_api_key from auth/keys — same-branch handler cross-package import needed for usage tracking with API key context
Imports load_json/save_json from json/json_handler — same-branch handler cross-package import for config file operations
False positive — get_google_service() has full Google-style docstring but multi-line signature (6 lines) exceeds checker's 5-line lookahead window
json_handler.py is the system-wide default handler — standard name used across all 15 branches, cannot be renamed without breaking all imports
False positive — effective_scopes is a local variable inside load_credentials() and run_oauth_flow(), not a module-level constant
False positive — wait is a local variable inside api_call_with_retry() loop body, not a module-level constant
Public API exports for cross-branch consumption (get_drive_service, authenticate_google, validate_google, reauth_google). Tested in test_google_client.py. Backup migration pending.
make_call() is a CLI orchestrator — parses args then delegates to client.get_response(). All business logic lives in handlers/openrouter/client.py.

Integration drivers live in apps/integrations/{project}/driver.py — gitignored private space intentionally outside the 3-layer structure. Loaded via importlib, not standard package import. See DPLAN-0133.
Test file — lives in tests/ by convention, not in the 3-layer app structure. Test files are exempt from layer architecture standard.

Integration driver test fixture in gitignored apps/integrations/ — intentionally outside 3-layer structure per DPLAN-0133 architecture. Loaded via importlib.util.spec_from_file_location, not standard package import.

Integration driver test fixture in gitignored apps/integrations/ — intentionally outside 3-layer structure per DPLAN-0133 architecture. Loaded via importlib.util.spec_from_file_location, not standard package import.

Integration driver test fixture in gitignored apps/integrations/ — intentionally outside 3-layer structure per DPLAN-0133 architecture. Loaded via importlib.util.spec_from_file_location, not standard package import.

fetch_contracts() and call_contract() are test API hooks called from tests/test_integrations.py. The unused_function checker excludes test dirs from its search corpus, so these appear unused despite being actively called. Encapsulation standard requires tests to go through modules, not import handlers directly — these functions serve that purpose.

notes.usage: Add entries to 'bypass' list to exclude specific violations

example.reason: Circular dependency - logger cannot import CLI

fields.standard: Standard name: cli, imports, naming, etc. (required)
fields.lines: Optional - specific line numbers to bypass
